title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, marking his second collaboration with the orchestra.
The concert featured a blend of American music, including pieces from Broadway and jazz standards, showcasing Odom Jr.'s versatility.
Highlights included Odom Jr.'s renditions of 'Wait for It' from Hamilton and Sam Cooke's 'A Change is Gonna Come'.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, faces multiple charges for allegedly assaulting two young children.
He allegedly burned a 2-year-old with a lighter and a 3-year-old with a heated fork, per prosecutors.
Bautista-Zepeda fled to Virginia after being confronted; he was taken into custody earlier this month.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, of Concord, was convicted of raping a woman in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
He received a prison sentence of 13.5 to 15 years for rape and five years probation for assault.
The court stated Jammal acted with 'a shocking sense of entitlement,' and he was not intoxicated during the incident.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word will be converted into condos following city approval.
Habitech Communities purchased the property in 2024 for $4 million and will develop 32 condo units.
The project aligns with a Greater Boston trend of converting churches into residential spaces.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Celtics, offering defensive prowess and versatility.
LeBron James, a top unrestricted free agent, could be a fit for the Celtics despite being a long shot.
John Collins, a forward, brings scoring ability and could add depth to the Celtics' roster.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time, defeating Molloy University in a three-game series.
Endicott College reached the NCAA Division III World Series for the fourth consecutive year after beating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott await their championship round opponents and game schedules, with competitions in North Carolina and Ohio.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics plan to work out UConn forward Alex Karaban next week ahead of the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, a Southborough native, ranks No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects list; Celtics have No. 27 and No. 40 picks.
Karaban averaged 12.5 points, 5.0 rebounds, and 2.0 assists over 151 games at UConn, winning two NCAA championships.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, showcasing his blend of jazz, pop, and older hits.
Puth's performance included live reimaginings of songs from his album, 'Whatever’s Clever!', highlighting his musicianship over stage spectacle.
The concert featured opening acts by Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey, who impressed the audience with their musical talents.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their entire 'Bringing Down the Horse' album at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
Jakob Dylan's band also played Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' album, highlighting their appreciation for Petty's music.
The concert concluded with encores including 'I've Been Delivered' and Petty classics 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting'.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston saw the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents last year, totaling a loss of 5,644 since 2020.
Everett and Worcester had the most significant population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively, according to U.S. Census data.
Statewide population growth slowed to 0.2% with 15,500 new residents, down from nearly 1% the previous year, impacted by housing costs and immigration policies.
